parentQuestion: Feeling a gentle caress on your arm would likely involve all of the following EXCEPT ___.

scaffolds.q: Do different receptors in the skin detect different types of touch?
options: No, all touch receptors are the same
Yes, different receptors detect different types of touch
Only one type of receptor exists
scaffolds.explain: The skin contains MULTIPLE types of mechanoreceptors — some for light touch, others for deep pressure, others for vibration. Specialization enables nuanced sensation.

wrongExplains.0: Skin has diverse receptor types. Each specializes in different mechanical stimuli.
wrongExplains.2: Skin is densely populated with receptors — that's why it's so sensitive.
wrongExplains.3: Multiple receptor types exist, each tuned to different stimuli.

scaffolds.q: What do Meissner's corpuscles detect and where are they located?
options: Light touch, in SUPERFICIAL skin layers
scaffolds.explain: MEISSNER'S CORPUSCLES are near the skin SURFACE and are very sensitive to LIGHT TOUCH and texture. A gentle caress would activate them.

wrongExplains.0: Deep pressure activates Pacinian corpuscles. Meissner's detect light touch superficially.
wrongExplains.2: Temperature is detected by thermoreceptors. Meissner's are touch receptors in skin.
wrongExplains.3: Sound is detected in the ears. Meissner's corpuscles are skin touch receptors.

options: Light, sustained touch in superficial skin
scaffolds.explain: MERKEL DISCS are in the basal epidermis and respond to SUSTAINED LIGHT TOUCH. A hand resting on your arm would activate them.

wrongExplains.0: Vibration is detected by Pacinian corpuscles. Merkel discs detect sustained light touch.
wrongExplains.2: Deep pressure activates Pacinian corpuscles. Merkel discs are superficial.
wrongExplains.3: Pain is detected by nociceptors. Merkel discs detect gentle sustained touch.

scaffolds.q: What do hair follicle receptors detect?
scaffolds.explain: Free nerve endings wrap around hair follicles. Even if a caress doesn't touch skin directly, BENDING THE HAIRS triggers these receptors.

wrongExplains.0: Blood pressure is monitored by baroreceptors. Hair follicle receptors detect hair movement.
wrongExplains.2: Temperature is detected by thermoreceptors. Hair receptors detect mechanical bending.
wrongExplains.3: Organ stretch is detected by interoceptors. Hair follicle receptors are in skin.

options: DEEP in the skin (dermis and subcutaneous tissue)
scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES are in the DEEP dermis and subcutaneous tissue. They're positioned to detect deep pressure and vibration, NOT gentle surface touch.

wrongExplains.0: Surface receptors include Meissner's. Pacinian corpuscles are deep.
wrongExplains.2: Pacinian corpuscles are peripheral receptors in skin and joints, not brain.
wrongExplains.3: Lungs have stretch receptors. Pacinian corpuscles are in skin and joints.

scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES require significant pressure to deform them. They detect DEEP PRESSURE and VIBRATION. A gentle caress wouldn't reach deep enough to activate them.

wrongExplains.0: Light touch activates Meissner's and Merkel's. Pacinian need stronger stimuli.
wrongExplains.2: Temperature is detected by thermoreceptors. Pacinian detect mechanical stimuli.
wrongExplains.3: Pain is detected by nociceptors. Pacinian detect vibration and pressure.

scaffolds.q: Would a gentle caress activate Pacinian corpuscles?
options: No, they are too deep for gentle surface touch
scaffolds.explain: A gentle caress activates SUPERFICIAL receptors (Meissner's, Merkel, hair follicles) but NOT DEEP receptors (Pacinian). Depth matters!

wrongExplains.0: Pacinian require significant pressure. Surface touch doesn't reach them.
wrongExplains.2: Speed doesn't change the depth. Gentle touch simply can't reach Pacinian corpuscles.
wrongExplains.3: Location doesn't matter — Pacinian corpuscles are deep everywhere.

scaffolds.q: Why does receptor location (superficial vs deep) matter?
options: Location determines what type of touch it responds to
scaffolds.explain: Receptor DEPTH = stimulus TYPE. Superficial receptors catch light touch. Deep receptors require more pressure. ARCHITECTURE MIRRORS FUNCTION.

wrongExplains.0: Location absolutely matters — it determines what stimuli reach the receptor.
wrongExplains.2: Deep receptors work perfectly — they just need deeper stimuli.
wrongExplains.3: Superficial receptors function normally and detect light touch.

scaffolds.q: Which receptor would NOT be activated by a gentle caress?
scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES are in deep tissue. A gentle caress doesn't penetrate deep enough. They detect vibration and deep pressure, not surface caresses.

wrongExplains.0: Meissner's corpuscles detect light touch — a caress would activate them.
wrongExplains.1: Merkel discs detect sustained light touch — a caress would activate them.
wrongExplains.2: Hair follicle receptors detect hair bending — a caress would activate them.
